title: First French-speaking regional group of network forms in Lausanne
description: The gay professionals' organization network gains its first French-speaking regional group, based in Lausanne.

title: Students launch the coming-out group HALLOWELT! at three Swiss cantonal schools
description: Cantonal-school students Ivo Colombo of Aarau along with Ruben Ott of Baden start HALLOWELT!, a coming-out support group active at the schools of Baden, Aarau, as well as Olten.

title: Zurich coming-out group Spätzünder relaunches as gay: my way
description: The Spätzünder group of the Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Zürich (HAZ) takes the new name gay: my way, redefining itself as a coming-out support space open to people of all ages.

title: Homologay in Neuchâtel dissolves
description: The Neuchâtel group Homologay formally disbands on 30 January 2006.

title: Gay-lesbian group Imbarco immediato starts in Ticino
description: Imbarco immediato, a group for gays as well as lesbians, forms in the Italian-speaking canton of Ticino on 11 February 2006.

title: ILGA holds its world conference in Geneva
description: The International Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, Trans and Intersex Association (ILGA) convenes its world conference in Geneva from 27 March to 3 April 2006.

title: Bernhard Pulver is the first openly gay man elected to a Swiss cantonal government
description: Green politician Bernhard Pulver wins election to the cantonal executive of Bern on 9 April 2006, making him the first openly gay man on any Swiss cantonal government.

title: Theres Bachofen becomes the first female president of the HAZ
description: The Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Zürich (HAZ) elect Theres Bachofen as president on 27 April 2006, the first woman to lead the organization.

title: Zurich CSD 2006 focuses on sex education in schools
description: Zurich's Christopher Street Day takes place from 9 to 11 June 2006 under the motto "Akzeptanz macht Schule!", putting classroom education about homosexuality on the agenda.

title: Checkpoint health center for men who have sex with men opens in Zurich
description: Checkpoint Zürich opens on 12 June 2006 as Switzerland's second dedicated health center serving gay men along with other men who have sex with men.

title: Swiss school-education groups merge into the Fachgruppe Bildung
description: On 5 July 2006 the school-project groups of Pink Cross, LOS, fels along with HAZ join forces as the Fachgruppe Bildung; the effort later operates as Eduqueer, then as Lehrplan Q under Pink Cross.

title: National Lesbian & Gay Pride takes place in Lausanne
description: Switzerland's national Lesbian & Gay Pride runs in Lausanne from 6 to 9 July 2006 under the bilingual theme "Tout près de chez vous". The event ends with a large deficit, so no pride is held in 2007.

title: National campaign group Ja zur Partnerschaft disbands
description: The national association Ja zur Partnerschaft, formed to campaign for the Swiss registered-partnership law, dissolves on 15 September 2006 after achieving its goal.

title: Bern film festival Queersicht marks its tenth anniversary
description: Queersicht, the lesbian-gay film festival of Bern, celebrates ten years with its 2006 edition from 9 to 12 November.

title: Switzerland's registered partnership law takes effect
description: The Swiss federal law on registered partnership for same-sex couples enters into force on 1 January 2007, letting couples nationwide register their partnership with legal effect.

title: Wankdorf Junxx, Switzerland's first gay-lesbian football fan club, forms in Bern
description: Supporters of BSC Young Boys launch the Wankdorf Junxx in Bern on 1 February 2007, the first gay-lesbian football fan club in Switzerland.

title: Youth group Happy Gays continues LGBTQ organizing in Neuchâtel
description: The youth group Happy Gays forms in Neuchâtel on 24 February 2007, taking over from the dissolved group Homologay.

title: Organizing committee Europride09 forms in Zurich
description: The association Europride09 is set up on 23 April 2007 to prepare the Europride scheduled for Zurich in May–June 2009.

title: National HIV/Aids association LHIVE forms in Switzerland
description: LHIVE, the Swiss national association of people living with HIV or Aids, is launched on 5 May 2007.

title: Film festival Pink Apple holds its tenth edition
description: Pink Apple, the gay-lesbian film festival of Frauenfeld along with Zurich, runs for the tenth time from 11 to 19 May 2007.

title: Zurich CSD 2007 marches under the motto Bekenne dich!
description: Zurich's Christopher Street Day takes place from 1 to 3 June 2007 with the motto "Bekenne dich!", a call to stand by one's identity.

title: Züri-Fest includes an official gay village for the first time
description: At the Züri-Fest city festival from 6 to 8 July 2007, a gay village is part of the official program — a first for a mainstream mass event of this size in Zurich.

title: First Swiss open-air festival for lesbians as well as gays held in Meilen
description: Switzerland's first open-air festival aimed at lesbians along with gay men takes place in Meilen, canton of Zurich, on 14 July 2007.

title: Swiss LGBTI police association pink cop forms in Zurich
description: Police officers found pink cop on 23 February 2008, a Swiss association of police-corps members, today known as pinkcop – LGBTI Police Switzerland, headquartered in Zurich.

title: Zurich CSD 2008 plays on the UEFA European Championship
description: Zurich's Christopher Street Day runs from 30 May to 1 June 2008 under the motto "offside & mittendrin", a nod to the football European Championship held shortly afterwards.

title: Lesbian & Gay Pride takes place in Biel
description: The Lesbian & Gay Pride in Biel on 21–22 June 2008 carries the bilingual motto "ensemble – miteinander".

title: Magazine akut ends after 25 issues; display succeeds it
description: In summer 2008 the magazine akut ceases publication after 25 issues; its successor appears under the name display.

title: Basel gets its first gay film festival, Luststreifen
description: Luststreifen, the first gay film festival in Basel, runs from 11 to 14 September 2008.

title: Helpline RainbowLine starts recording reports of homophobic violence
description: The Swiss counselling helpline RainbowLine expands its role in 2008, additionally serving as a reporting point for incidents of homophobic violence.

title: Queer group Queerdom forms in Schaffhausen
description: Queerdom, a new group for lesbian, gay, bi as well as trans people, starts up in Schaffhausen in 2008.

title: Last Swiss regional group of the HuK network dissolves in Bern
description: The Bern group, last remaining Swiss regional group of the ecumenical HuK network (Homosexuals in the church), disbands on 10 February 2009.

title: Europride 2009 opens in Zurich
description: The Europride09 festival opens in Zurich on 2 May 2009, starting a month of Europe-wide pride programming in the city.

title: Online archive schwulengeschichte.ch goes live
description: The website schwulengeschichte.ch, documenting the history of gay life in Switzerland, launches with a public vernissage on 3 June 2009.

title: Europride parade draws around 100,000 people to Zurich
description: The main days of Europride09 run from 4 to 7 June 2009, with a demonstration march through central Zurich plus festivities on Münsterhof square attended by roughly 100,000 visitors.

title: First Swiss LGBT youth conference convenes in Bern
description: Switzerland's first national LGBT youth conference takes place in Bern on 24 April 2010.

title: Pink Cross along with LOS move into shared offices in Bern
description: The national gay organization Pink Cross plus the lesbian organization LOS move into joint premises at Monbijoustrasse 73 in Bern on 17 May 2010.

title: Zurich CSD rebrands as the Zurich Pride Festival
description: The former Christopher Street Day adopts the name Zurich Pride Festival, holding its 2010 edition from 3 to 6 June under the motto "Do ask – Do tell".

title: Petition demands equal rights for registered couples' families
description: The petition "Familienchancen", backed by 19,337 signatures, is handed in at Bern on 15 June 2010. It calls for registered same-sex couples along with their children to have the same rights as married couples' families.

title: Hugues Cuénod, oldest openly gay man in the world, turns 105
description: Geneva tenor Hugues Cuénod, regarded as the world's oldest openly gay man, celebrates his 105th birthday on 26 June 2010. In January he registered his partnership with his longtime partner, making him the oldest person worldwide to do so; he dies on 3 December 2010.

title: Transgender Network Switzerland is launched
description: Harry Hohmann starts the Transgender Network Switzerland (TGNS) in 2010, giving trans people in Switzerland a national advocacy organization.

title: Zurich Pride Festival 2011 embraces difference
description: The Zurich Pride Festival runs from 17 to 19 June 2011 under the bilingual motto "Live with the difference – vivre avec la différence".

title: Liechtenstein approves registered partnership by referendum
description: Voters in Liechtenstein accept a registered-partnership law modeled on Switzerland's with 68.8% approval on 19 June 2011. Liechtenstein thereby becomes the second country worldwide to introduce such a law by popular vote, while being among the last in Europe to adopt one.

title: National Council committee rejects the Familienchancen petition
description: The legal-affairs committee of the Swiss National Council votes 13 to 10 against the petition "Familienchancen" on 23 June 2011.

title: Council of States committee backs adoption rights for registered couples
description: On 20 October 2011 the legal-affairs committee of the Swiss Council of States endorses the petition "Familienchancen", filing a motion titled "Gleiche Chancen für alle Familien" that asks the government to allow adoption within the partnership law.

title: Book about the couple Röbi Rapp as well as Ernst Ostertag launches
description: Barbara Bosshard's book "Verborgene Liebe", telling the life story of the couple Röbi Rapp plus Ernst Ostertag, premieres at its launch event on 3 November 2011.

title: Queer youth-platform association, later Milchjugend, is set up
description: An association promoting a queer youth platform forms on 8 February 2012; it later takes the name Milchjugend.

title: HAZ youth group Tankschtell starts in Zurich
description: The Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Zürich (HAZ) relaunch their youth group on 5 May 2012 under the name Tankschtell, meant as a place where young queer people can recharge among themselves.

title: Basel gay working groups meet Bishop Felix Gmür
description: The Homosexuelle Arbeitsgruppen Basel (HABS) hold talks with Catholic bishop Felix Gmür on 28 June 2012, the first meeting of its kind in the diocese of Basel.

title: Swiss chapter of the It Gets Better project is launched
description: Following the US It Gets Better model, activists set up a Swiss association on 24 November 2012 that supports coming-out, among other things through video messages.

title: National Council backs stepchild adoption for registered couples
description: On 13 December 2012 the Swiss National Council supports the motion "Gleiche Chancen für alle Familien" but narrows it, allowing only the adoption of a registered partner's stepchildren rather than full adoption rights.
